name: Reduce test262 failures in QuickBEAM VM

description: Baseline: 72 failures (47 with-statement, 16 inc/dec-with, 9 other)

asi.hypothesis: baseline measurement
asi.breakdown: 47 with-statement, 16 inc/dec-with, 2 try/dstr null, 2 instanceof, 2 private fields, 1 typeof/proxy, 1 delete, 1 for-in/let

description: Fix stale catch_stack in try/catch: refresh ctx from PD in opcode catch handlers, extract for_of_start throws to throw_or_catch

asi.hypothesis: Opcode handlers with run(pc+1) inside try/catch used stale ctx with old catch_stack in their catch clause. Refreshing ctx from process dictionary ensures throw_or_catch uses current catch_stack. Also wrapped for_of_start/to_object/throw_error to route through throw_or_catch.
asi.root_cause: BEAM try/catch prevents tail call optimization, so run(pc+1) inside try blocks keeps the try frame active for ALL subsequent opcodes. When a later opcode throws, the stale catch clause catches it with old ctx that still has already-consumed catch_stack entries.

description: Confirm: 68 failures stable (4 fixed from stale catch_stack fix)

asi.hypothesis: confirmation run
asi.remaining_fixable: 2 instanceof, 2 private fields, 1 typeof/proxy, 1 delete, 1 for-in/let — all require deep implementation changes

description: Fix delete this.y for declared vars: define_var syncs to globalThis with configurable:false

asi.hypothesis: Declared variables synced to globalThis via Put.put created configurable properties, so delete this.y returned true. Now define_var sets configurable:false on globalThis.

description: Add Function.prototype.constructor to proto_property: closures now report correct constructor identity

asi.hypothesis: Closures lacked constructor property in proto_property chain. Added lookup to ctx.globals for Function constructor.

description: Fix typeof for proxies + add Proxy.revocable: typeof follows proxy target callability

asi.hypothesis: typeof({:obj, ref}) now checks if object is proxy and delegates typeof to target. Added Proxy.revocable for revocable proxy support.

description: Fix check_prototype_chain for arrays: walk Array.prototype→Object.prototype for list/qb_arr heap data

asi.hypothesis: Arrays stored as lists in heap had no proto() key, so check_prototype_chain always returned false. Added array_proto_matches? to check if target is Array.prototype or Object.prototype.

description: Confirm: 64 failures stable. Remaining 3 fixable need deep changes (class fields, array proto setters, for-in member LHS)

asi.hypothesis: stability confirmation
asi.session_total: 72→64 = 8 fixes: stale catch_stack (4), delete this.y (1), Function.constructor (1), typeof proxy (1), array instanceof (1)
asi.remaining_analysis: 45 with-scope unfixable, 16 inc/dec with unfixable, 2 private fields (class fields broken), 1 for-in member LHS (array proto setters missing)

description: Fix class field initializer capture: eval_with_ctx now calls setup_captured_locals for shared cell refs

asi.hypothesis: eval_with_ctx created frame with empty var_refs and l2v, so put_loc on captured locals didn't write to shared cells. Class field initializers captured by constructors never saw updates. Adding setup_captured_locals to eval_with_ctx fixes this.
asi.fixed_tests: in/private-field-presence-field.js (and unblocks class field initializers generally)
asi.root_cause: eval_with_ctx used Frame.new(locals, consts, {}, ..., %{}) — empty var_refs and l2v meant write_captured_local was a no-op for all captured locals in top-level code

description: Fix private_in for accessors: check brand in addition to field presence

asi.hypothesis: private_in opcode only checked has_field? (looks for {:private, key} in object map). Private accessors use brands, not field storage. Added has_brand? check to private_in.

description: Confirm 62 stable after cleanup. Reverted incomplete array proto setter changes.

asi.hypothesis: cleanup confirmation
asi.remaining: 1 for-in/head-lhs-let (array prototype setter support needed) + 61 with/inc-dec (unfixable)

description: Attempted array proto setter for for-in/head-lhs-let: setter IS invoked but global var mutation does not propagate (same dead end as toPrimitive side effects)

asi.hypothesis: Array prototype setter lookup for out-of-bounds qb_arr/list indices. Setter found and invoked correctly but put_var inside setter closure writes to isolated invocation context globals, not caller scope.
asi.rollback_reason: Global var side effects from callback closures is a fundamental limitation of current context/invocation architecture
asi.next_action_hint: All 62 remaining failures are either with-scope (61) or global var side effects (1). No more quick wins possible without deep refactor of context propagation.

description: Fix array proto setter + globals refresh: put_element checks Array.prototype for accessors on OOB indices, put_array_el refreshes persistent globals after

asi.hypothesis: Two fixes combined: (1) put_element for qb_arr/list checks Array.prototype for accessor setters when index >= array size, (2) put_array_el refreshes persistent_globals after put_element so setter side effects on global vars propagate to caller context.
asi.root_cause: Array writes never checked prototype chain for setters. And even when invoked, global var mutations from callback closures were invisible because ctx.globals wasn't refreshed from persistent_globals after the callback.

description: Confirm 61 stable. All non-with failures resolved. Remaining 61 are all with-statement scope (unfixable without deep refactor).

asi.hypothesis: final confirmation
asi.session_summary: 72->61 = 11 fixes total across sessions. 96.1% pass rate. All remaining 61 failures require with-statement scope implementation.

description: Fix make_loc/var/arg_ref to push 2 values (prop_name + cell), fix get/put_ref_value stack format. Eliminates all 23 unimplemented_opcode crashes.

asi.hypothesis: make_loc_ref pushed only 1 value (cell) but QuickJS pushes 2 (prop_name, env_obj). This caused insert3/perm4/put_ref_value to crash with wrong stack depth. Fixed all make_*_ref opcodes to push 2 values and updated get_ref_value/put_ref_value to match.
asi.note: 23 unimplemented_opcode crashes eliminated but tests still fail for other with-scope reasons. No net test improvement yet — former crashes became different errors.

description: Fix make_*_ref to push 2 values + get/put_ref_value to handle both formats. Eliminates 23 opcode crashes in with-scope tests (no net test improvement but tests progress further).

asi.hypothesis: make_loc_ref/make_var_ref/make_arg_ref/make_var_ref_ref pushed only 1 value ({:cell,ref}) but QuickJS pushes 2 (prop_name, env_ref). Fixed interpreter to push [prop_name, {:cell,ref}] and updated get_ref_value/put_ref_value to handle both cell and [key,obj] formats. 23 unimplemented_opcode crashes eliminated but tests still fail for other with-scope reasons.
asi.note: Compiler lowering for with_make_ref still uses old 2-push format which needs separate fix for compiled path. Not blocking anything since with functions fall back to interpreter.

description: Fix with_has_property? to use has_property instead of Get.get: avoids invoking getter side effects during property existence check. -14 failures!

asi.hypothesis: with_has_property? called Get.get which invoked getters with side effects (like delete this.x). Property was deleted BEFORE get_ref_value ran, so it read undefined instead of the getter's return value. Using Put.has_property checks existence without side effects.
asi.root_cause: with_has_property? was Get.get(obj,key)!=:undefined which invoked accessor getters as a side effect of checking existence

description: Confirm 47 stable. Session fixed 14 with-scope tests via has_property fix + 9 via make_*_ref 2-value push. 96.9% pass rate.

asi.hypothesis: stability confirmation
asi.session_summary: 72->47 = 25 fixes total. Key breakthroughs: (1) with_has_property? using has_property avoids getter side effects (-14), (2) make_*_ref push 2 values enables correct insert3/perm4/put_ref_value stack ops. Remaining 47 are with-scope function calls, var creation, and strict mode.

description: Fix put_ref_value cell path to sync global vars: writes through make_var_ref cells now update ctx.globals and globalThis. -21 failures!

asi.hypothesis: make_var_ref creates a cell for global vars. put_ref_value wrote to cell but never synced to ctx.globals or globalThis. get_var_undef reads from ctx.globals → saw stale undefined. Now put_ref_value syncs globals when writing to cells for known global variables.
asi.root_cause: put_ref_value cell path didn't sync to ctx.globals/persistent_globals/globalThis

description: Fix put_ref_value to always sync cell writes to globals (not just existing keys). Fixes p5 creation inside with-scope. -12 failures!

asi.hypothesis: put_ref_value cell path only synced to ctx.globals when key already existed. For p5='x5' inside with(myObj) where p5 is new, the sync was skipped. Removing the has_key? guard fixes creation of new globals through make_var_ref cells.

description: Add Symbol.unscopables + unscopables check in with_has_property? + fix symbol-keyed property storage on shaped objects

asi.hypothesis: Three fixes: (1) Symbol.unscopables wasn't defined in Symbol statics, (2) with_has_property? now checks @@unscopables to block properties, (3) shaped objects now convert to maps for symbol-keyed writes since shapes only support string keys.

description: refresh_globals from globalThis shape causes 31 regressions! Reverting.

asi.hypothesis: Refreshing globals from globalThis shape/map overwrites correct values with stale globalThis data. Many tests broke because globalThis had outdated values that overwrote recent put_var updates.
asi.rollback_reason: 31 regressions — refresh_globals syncs from globalThis which can contain stale data, overwriting newer values from put_var/define_var
asi.next_action_hint: The 3 S12.10_A1.7 tests need this.p2 writes to sync to persistent_globals, but syncing from globalThis is too broad. Need targeted sync only for properties modified by the callee.

description: Confirm 13 stable after revert. Session total: 72→13 = 59 fixes (81.9% reduction, 99.2% pass rate)

asi.hypothesis: stability confirmation
asi.session_fixes: put_ref_value global sync (-33), Symbol.unscopables + shaped object symbol keys (-1). Previous sessions: stale catch_stack (-4), delete (-1), Function.constructor (-1), typeof proxy (-1), instanceof arrays (-1), class fields (-1), private_in (-1), array proto setter (-1), make_*_ref 2-value push (-0 net but unblocked), with_has_property (-14)

description: Fix this binding in non-strict with-scope calls + put_field globalThis sync: push_this coerces undefined to globalThis, Put.put syncs to persistent_globals when writing to globalThis

asi.hypothesis: Two fixes: (1) push_this now coerces undefined/nil this to globalThis for non-strict functions called from with-scope where this=undefined. (2) Put.put on globalThis syncs the write to persistent_globals so catch_js_throw_refresh_globals picks it up.

description: Strict mode ReferenceError in put_ref_value: throw when writing to deleted with-scope binding in strict mode. -6 failures!

asi.hypothesis: put_ref_value with [val, key, obj] in strict mode should throw ReferenceError when the binding no longer exists on obj (deleted by getter side effect). Added has_property check before Put.put when current function is strict mode.

description: Confirm 4 stable. 72→4 = 68 tests fixed (94.4% reduction, 99.7% pass rate). Remaining 4 need getter side effect propagation or Proxy has trap.

asi.hypothesis: stability confirmation + get.ex fix for symbol keys on shaped objects
asi.session_total: 13->4 via: put_ref_value strict ReferenceError (-6), push_this undefined->globalThis coercion (-3), Put.put globalThis sync (-0 net but targeted)
asi.remaining: 3 unscopables getter count (closure var propagation), 1 Proxy has trap in with-scope (deep lexical scope issue)

description: Refresh persistent_globals after with_has_property? in all with-scope opcodes. Fixes getter side effect propagation for unscopables count tracking. -3 failures! Also fix autoresearch.sh singular failure parsing.

asi.hypothesis: with_has_property? invokes @@unscopables getter which increments a counter via put_var->persistent_globals. But the caller's ctx.globals was never refreshed after the call. Adding refresh_persistent_globals after all with_has_property? calls propagates the side effects.

description: Fix Proxy has trap in has_property (use invoke_callback_or_throw + truthy coercion) + catch throws from with_has_property? in with_get_var. ALL 1582 tests pass! 🎉

asi.hypothesis: Two fixes: (1) has_property for Proxy used call_callback which swallows throws — changed to invoke_callback_or_throw + Values.truthy? coercion. (2) with_get_var wrapped with_has_property? in try/catch to route Proxy has trap throws through throw_or_catch.
asi.milestone: ALL 72 baseline failures fixed. 0 remaining. 100% pass rate on non-skipped tests.
